faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about D-Secure Drive Eraser

faq.q: How many drives can I erase at a time on one machine?
faq.a: D-Secure Drive Eraser supports simultaneous erasure of up to 32 drives per machine, depending on your hardware configuration. For bulk operations, you can use our cloud console to manage multiple machines.

faq.q: Does the software support Partition Deletion Wipe?
faq.a: Yes, D-Secure supports partition-level erasure in addition to full drive erasure. You can selectively wipe specific partitions while keeping others intact.
No, D-Secure is designed with a user-friendly interface. Simply boot from USB, select your drive and erasure standard, and the software handles everything automatically.

faq.q: Can I sign my file, like XML, jar file with my e-Signature?
faq.a: Yes, D-Secure supports custom digital signatures for reports. You can integrate your organization's PKI for enhanced security.
Yes, D-Secure offers native integration with ServiceNow via REST APIs. You can automatically update CMDB records and create incident tickets upon erasure completion.

faq.q: Can I track separate Inventory for different types of Drives (SSD/HDD, Mobile, etc)?
faq.a: Absolutely. The cloud console provides detailed categorization and filtering by device type, making it easy to track and report on different asset categories.
We provide continuous support including regular software updates, technical assistance, compliance monitoring, and renewal coordination. Think of us as your ongoing partner in data hygiene.

blog_overwrite-guide_excerpt: A deep-dive analysis of NIST 800-88 and DoD 5220.22-M standards. A nuanced analysis of overwrite passes required for modern storage media versus legacy magnetic platters.
blog_ssd-wipe-guide_excerpt: Why traditional wiping methods fail on SSDs. Exploration of command-based erasure, cryptographic sanitization, and handling wear-leveling algorithms effectively.
blog_data-deletion-myths_excerpt: Formatting is not erasure. We expose common misconceptions that leave organizations vulnerable to data breaches and regulatory fines.
blog_best-data-erasure-methods_title: Best Data Erasure Method for Any Storage Media Type
blog_best-data-erasure-methods_excerpt: One size doesn't fit all. Learn the right erasure standard for HDDs, SSDs, and mobile devices to ensure compliance.
